issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring entirely for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 36px (within 24–43px range) but padding is only var(--space-1-5) (6px) which is below the 8px compensation threshold. The effective touch target may be smaller than the recommended 44×44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target at minimum size with no padding compensation on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px (exactly at the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum) with no explicit padding to increase the touch target area. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small targets.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Screen reader only utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto instead of absolute positioning and clipping. This makes the content visible to sighted users and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with no padding on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px (within 24–43px range) with padding: 0. Without compensating padding, the 28px touch target is below the recommended 44×44px and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes have explicit width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24×24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). This makes them difficult or impossible for some users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset to -3px without restoring a visible outline. The negative offset alone does not create a visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-color without restoring the outline width. No visible focus ring is rendered.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button class includes transform in its transition property (line 530), which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on headings
issuesFound.explanation: Heading elements h1-h6 use letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which evaluates to -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing reduces text legibility and can make content difficult to read for users with low vision or dyslexia.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on .price-amount
issuesFound.explanation: The price amount uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) which evaluates to -0.05em. This tight negative spacing significantly reduces readability of numerical content.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset. Offset does not create a visible ring on a zero-width outline, removing keyboard focus indication entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 333: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Screen reader-only content will be visible on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses a transform transition without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable it, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.fix: Line 1494: .footer-brand address a { color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); display: inline; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph in .section-header uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and provides only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius. The outline-offset property alone does not create a visible focus ring, and no box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline replacement is provided. Keyboard users will be unable to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size. While padding is present, the explicit min-height may result in touch targets smaller than 44px on certain devices or when padding does not sufficiently expand the interactive area.

issuesFound.title: Touch target at minimum size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but is below the recommended 44px touch target size. Without additional padding to expand the interactive area, these links may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator for buttons when focused via keyboard. This removes the browser default focus ring without providing a replacement.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Content marked with this class remains visible to sighted users while also failing to be announced properly by screen readers in all contexts. The class should use position: absolute with 1px dimensions and clip properties.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit dimensions of 28px × 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the recommended 44×44px touch target size. With no compensating padding to expand the interactive area to 44px, users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm modifier sets min-height: 36px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size. Small buttons may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset to -3px without restoring a visible outline style or width. This utility class fails to provide a visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-color without specifying an outline style or width. Without an outline width and style, no visible focus ring is rendered.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has explicit dimensions of 20px × 20px, which is below the minimum 24×24px touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the checkbox difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion, which may trigger vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector defines a transition that includes transform, causing spatial movement on hover/focus. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .required-note
issuesFound.explanation: The .required-note uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background (var(--colour-bg-page)), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text in .features-section .section-header uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, failing WCAG AA requirements for normal text.

issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-brand address
issuesFound.explanation: The address in .footer-brand uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against the dark background #0f172a, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.3:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-brand address a
issuesFound.explanation: The links in .footer-brand address use var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against the dark background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.2:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links use var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against the dark background var(--colour-neutral-900) (#0f172a), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.2:1, failing WCAG AA for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: The copyright text in .footer-bottom uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against the dark background #0f172a, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.3:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p a
issuesFound.explanation: The links in .footer-bottom use var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against the dark background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.2:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The social links use var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against the dark footer background #0f172a, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.2:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on main navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a selector sets text-decoration: none and relies solely on colour change (to var(--colour-brand-primary)) to distinguish links from surrounding text on hover. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color as colour alone is not sufficient to identify links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector sets text-decoration: none with no non-colour visual differentiator (hover only changes colour), failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets text-decoration: none with hover state only changing colour, not providing a non-colour visual differentiator required by WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets text-decoration: none with hover state only changing colour, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. This removes the focus ring entirely for keyboard users because outline-offset does not create a visible ring without a non-zero outline width. This affects all interactive elements relying on this global style.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. This removes the focus ring for button elements when focused via keyboard, making it impossible for users to identify the active element.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator relies on box-shadow in High Contrast Mode
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus selector sets outline: none and relies on box-shadow for the focus ring. Box-shadows are not visible in Windows High Contrast Mode, causing the focus indicator to disappear for users using this mode.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px. This is below the recommended 44px touch target size (WCAG 2.5.5), making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px. This is below the minimum 24px touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size Minimum), failing Level AA compliance and hindering activation for users with dexterity issues.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets a min-height of 32px. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets. The internal padding does not increase the total box size due to border-box sizing.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36px. This is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets, potentially causing activation errors for users on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes the content fully visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality of screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses a transform transition to slide into view. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 423 is empty and does not disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on #search-input::placeholder
issuesFound.explanation: The placeholder text color #94a3b8 on background #f8fafc has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.5:1, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. This makes the placeholder hard to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input::placeholder
issuesFound.explanation: The global placeholder text color #94a3b8 on the default white background has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum. This affects all inputs using this reset.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .features-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The text color #aaaaaa on white background has a contrast ratio of 2.8:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement. This text is difficult to read for users with moderate vision loss.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: The card body text uses color #aaaaaa on white, resulting in a 2.8:1 contrast ratio. This fails WCAG AA standards for readability.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .step-content p
issuesFound.explanation: The step description text uses color #aaaaaa on white, resulting in a 2.8:1 contrast ratio. This makes the content difficult to perceive for many users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .pricing-toggle > span
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle label text uses color #aaaaaa on white, resulting in a 2.8:1 contrast ratio. This fails the minimum contrast requirement for text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .plan-tagline
issuesFound.explanation: The plan tagline text uses color #aaaaaa on white, resulting in a 2.8:1 contrast ratio. This text is not sufficiently distinct from the background.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .price-period
issuesFound.explanation: The price period text uses color #aaaaaa on white, resulting in a 2.8:1 contrast ratio. This fails WCAG AA contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .logo-bar-label
issuesFound.explanation: The logo bar label text uses color #94a3b8 on background #f8fafc, resulting in a contrast ratio below 3:1. This text is very difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. Color alone is not sufficient to distinguish links from surrounding text for users with color vision deficiencies.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. This relies solely on color to indicate interactivity, which fails WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .footer-bottom > p a
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the footer bottom paragraph have text-decoration: none at rest. They only gain an underline on hover, meaning they are indistinguishable from text by non-color means at rest.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .feature-card a
issuesFound.explanation: The 'Learn more' links in feature cards have text-decoration: none at rest. While they gain an underline on hover, they rely on color alone to be identified as links in their default state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the outline on :focus-visible without providing a replacement. Box-shadows are suppressed in forced-colors mode, making focus indicators invisible for high-contrast mode users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360-363: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none with only an outline-offset property. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users with no indication of focus. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 556: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px exactly, which meets the minimum but falls short of the 44px recommended size for touch targets. Without additional padding, these links may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1419: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the visible focus indicator for button elements. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 492: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text will be visible to all users, disrupting the visual layout and potentially confusing users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 395-405: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size and lacks sufficient padding to compensate. While meeting the 24px minimum, the small size may still cause difficulty for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 802: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target requirement. This makes the button difficult or impossible for some users to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 568: `.btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Custom checkboxes must meet minimum size requirements to be accessible to users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This negative spacing can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by making word boundaries harder to distinguish.
issuesFound.fix: Line 163: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on global anchor elements
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration without providing a non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom or font-weight change. Links are only distinguished by colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 172: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add `a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button removes the outline in focus-visible state with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users on this critical interactive control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 705: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown submenu links ([id$="-submenu"] li a) remove the outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset, making keyboard navigation through dropdown menus invisible to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 771: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search form submit button sets outline: none with only outline-offset in focus-visible state, removing the focus indicator for keyboard users attempting to activate search.
issuesFound.fix: Line 809: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset, leaving keyboard users without visible indication of focus position in the account menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 820: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property without a prefers-reduced-motion override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the transform animation when buttons are interacted with.
issuesFound.fix: Line 476: Keep existing transition. Add after line 492: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes a transform transition for hover effects, and the empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 904-909 fails to disable this animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the 3D perspective transform.
issuesFound.fix: Line 904-909: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-slower); transform: none !important; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making content difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 631: `.section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on logo bar label
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label uses var(--colour-neutral-400) which is #94a3b8 against var(--colour-neutral-50) which is #f8fafc, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 916: `.logo-bar-label { text-align: center; font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-widest); margin-bottom: var(--space-8); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on feature card paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background, resulting in a 2.3:1 contrast ratio. This fails WCAG AA requirements and makes feature descriptions difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 990: `.feature-card p { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; flex-grow: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on step content paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The .step-content p uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a 2.3:1 contrast ratio. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for body text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1044: `.step-content p { font-size: var(--font-size-base); color: var(--colour-neutral-700); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on products section header
issuesFound.explanation: The .products-section .section-header p uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white, resulting in a 2.3:1 contrast ratio. This fails WCAG AA requirements for text readability.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. In forced-colors mode, box-shadow is suppressed, so the outline is the only reliable focus indicator. Removing it without replacement leaves no visible focus indicator for high-contrast mode users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 367: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus. The outline-offset property alone does not create a visible indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 356: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap at certain font sizes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 267: Remove the line or change to `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible only sets outline-offset: -3px. It inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule, resulting in no visible focus indicator for elements using this class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 376: `.focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible. This fails to visually hide content while keeping it in the accessibility tree; instead, it renders content visibly in the layout. Screen-reader-only text must use position: absolute with 1px dimensions and clip properties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 385: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has a transition on transform property (transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower)), causing spatial movement. No reduced-motion override exists for this element.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1126: `.plan-tagline { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for price period text
issuesFound.explanation: The .price-period selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background. The contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1 fails WCAG AA.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1138: `.price-period { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for testimonial author role
issuesFound.explanation: The .testimonial-card figcaption p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against the white card background. The contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1 fails the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1340: `.testimonial-card figcaption p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links (footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a) inherit text-decoration: none from the global rule and only provide a colour change on hover (from #64748b to #ffffff). Colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1439: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social links (.social-links a) inherit text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour change (from #64748b to #ffffff) to indicate interactivity. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color as there is no non-colour visual differentiator such as underline or border.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1460: `.social-links a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The base :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all elements relying on this base rule, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.
issuesFound.fix: Line 337: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule inside @media (forced-colors: active) sets outline: none. This suppresses the focus indicator specifically for users in High Contrast Mode, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348: outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px. Since outline-offset applies to a zero-width outline, no visible ring appears. Keyboard users cannot identify focused buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. This affects the mobile navigation toggle button, hiding focus state from keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 603: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none. Dropdown menu links lose their focus indicator, hindering keyboard navigation within menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 647: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none. While the :focus state has a box-shadow, the :focus-visible state (keyboard) does not, removing the indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 683: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none. The search submit button loses its focus ring for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 699: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account nav links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none. Account navigation links lose their focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 716: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px. This is below the 44px recommended target size (WCAG 2.5.8), making buttons difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 513: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. This is below the 24px minimum required size, creating a significant barrier for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 563: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px. Icon buttons must be at least 24x24px to be accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 573: min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle class sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended size for interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 593: min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on nav menu links
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a selector sets min-height: 28px. Navigation links should have a minimum height of 44px for easy activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 613: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a selector sets min-height: 28px. Dropdown links are too small for reliable touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 637: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input sets height: 28px. Form inputs should be at least 44px high to accommodate touch input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 663: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px. This button is too small for reliable touch activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 689: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on footer nav links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px. While this meets the absolute minimum, it is below the 44px recommended size for enhanced accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1478: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width: 20px and height: 20px. Custom checkboxes must be at least 24x24px to be accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1441: width: 24px; height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Elements intended for screen readers only will be visible to sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 367: position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search label.sr-only rule uses display: none. This hides the label from screen readers, making the search input inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 657: position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation. The global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, so this motion is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 443: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform. The global reduced-motion block is empty, so button transform animations persist for users who requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 443: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transform and transition. The specific @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 843 is empty, failing to disable the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 843: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector uses transition: transform. No reduced-motion override disables this specific animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 443: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.fix: Line 233: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator in high-contrast mode where box-shadow is also suppressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 355-358: Replace with `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users entirely, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 342-346: Replace with `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces spacing between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 175: Remove `word-spacing: -0.2em;` or set to `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border.
issuesFound.fix: Line 572-574: Add `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or change to `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen reader-only text remains visible in the layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 375-385: Replace with `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and has transform: translateY(1px) on :active, but lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:active { transform: none; transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 610: Change `min-height: 20px;` to `min-height: 24px;` (or preferably 44px)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector inherits text-decoration: none and only provides color change on hover, failing to provide a non-colour visual differentiator for links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 775: Add `text-decoration: underline;` or add `border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;` with `border-bottom-color: currentColor;` on hover

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for the mobile navigation toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 672-674: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 622-624: Change `min-width: 20px;` and `min-height: 20px;` to at least 24px

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without a visible replacement, removing focus indicators from dropdown menu links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 715-718: Change to `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px;`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 758-760: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with outline-offset: 0, removing the focus indicator. While a box-shadow exists on :focus, it is overridden by :focus-visible and box-shadow is invisible in forced-colors mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 745-747: Add `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1296: Change `width: 20px; height: 20px; min-height: 20px;` to at least 24px for all values

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has width: 28px and height: 28px (24-43px range) with padding: 0, falling below the 44px recommended target size without compensating spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 750-752: Increase width and height to 44px, or add min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav links have min-height: 24px with no padding, meeting the minimum but falling below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1340: Add `min-height: 44px;` or vertical padding to achieve 44px total height

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transition: transform and transform on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 815 is empty and does not disable the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 815-818: Add `.hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; transition: none; }` inside the media query

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on card link arrows
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a::after selector has transition: transform and transform: translateX(3px) on hover, with no reduced motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 883: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a::after { transition: none; transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector has transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01), but the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block only covers .pricing-card:hover, not the featured variant.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1136-1138: Add `.pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }` to the media query

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds aria-describedby linking to the error message, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1682: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the null check

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function toggles the mobile navigation drawer visible and adds CSS classes, but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is expanded. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2158: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after setting `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile navigation drawer and updates aria-expanded to "false", but unlike the open() function, it does not call liveRegion.announce() to notify screen reader users that the menu has closed. This creates an asymmetry where opening is announced but closing is silent.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2190: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }` before the emit call

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown menu panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but fails to set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The _closeInstance function correctly sets it to "false", leaving screen reader users unaware when the panel opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2284: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function collapses the FAQ answer and sets aria-expanded="false", but does not announce the collapsed state via the live region. The open() function announces "question — expanded", so screen reader users hear when content opens but not when it closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3800: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60))); }` before the emit call

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates CSS active classes for filter tab buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the pressed state. Since these buttons act as toggles (filter on/off), they must communicate their state to screen readers via aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3205: Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel and updates visual state, but never sets the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The close() function sets it to "false", so screen readers only hear state changes when closing, not opening.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3756: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not activate focus trap or move focus into drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the mobile navigation drawer but never activates the focus trap (_trap is declared but never assigned or activated) and does not programmatically move focus to the first focusable element inside the drawer. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button while the drawer opens behind them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` and `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class and announces the state change but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not know the menu is expanded when opened programmatically or via toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1665: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. This leaves the ARIA state out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1825: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel and announces the change but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger. Assistive technology will not reflect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2730: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions change visible content (showing/hiding panels) but do not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when dropdowns open or close.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1825: liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');
  Line 1840: liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput._onApply does not set aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the function announces the error but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. The input's invalid state is not exposed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3590: if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField._addFile does not set aria-invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: When a file fails validation in _addFile, the error is announced but aria-invalid is not set on the input element. The form field's invalid state is missing from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3860: setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button aria-pressed not initialised for reduced motion
issuesFound.explanation: In init, if prefersReducedMotion is true, play() returns early without setting aria-pressed on the play button. The button's state remains undefined for users with reduced motion preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3290: if (_playBtn) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register does not enforce role or tabindex on non-button triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The register function accepts a trigger element but does not ensure it has role="button" and tabindex="0" if it is not a native button. Custom elements used as triggers will be ignored by assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1915: if (trigger.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(trigger, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(trigger, 'tabindex', '0'); }

issuesFound.title: pricing.highlightPlan action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The highlightPlan function updates the visual state of a plan card (adding 'featured' class) but does not announce this change to screen readers. Users are unaware which plan is currently recommended.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3330: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.recommendedPlan || 'Plan highlighted as recommended');

issuesFound.title: markInvalid utility omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The helper function adds aria-describedby linking to error messages but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid form fields. Assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine the error state without this attribute, violating Success Criterion 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function, before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open() fails to set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When expanding an FAQ item, the function updates CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but neglects to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state, leaving users unaware that content is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation focus trap never initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(), leaving keyboard users able to tab outside the open navigation drawer into the underlying page content, violating modal dialog focus containment requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true, escapeDeactivates: true });` immediately after the `_initialised = true;` line in the init() function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown close via outside click loses focus
issuesFound.explanation: When closing dropdowns via outside click, the _closeAll function calls _closeInstance without the returnFocus parameter, causing focus to be lost instead of returning to the trigger button. Keyboard users expect focus to return to the element that opened the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change `_closeInstance(inst);` to `_closeInstance(inst, true);` inside the _closeAll function.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs lack aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons function as toggle buttons indicating the active category, but the JavaScript only toggles CSS classes without updating the aria-pressed attribute. Screen readers cannot convey which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` in the _syncTabAriaStates function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will announce the error description but not the invalid state itself, reducing clarity for users.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds the visible class to the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves the trigger state out of sync with the visible menu, confusing assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The trigger's state remains false while the menu is visible, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function expands the FAQ panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger. The trigger indicates collapsed state while the answer is visible, creating a contradiction for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator._animate does not announce final value
issuesFound.explanation: The _animate function updates the visible number but does not announce the final value to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on audio feedback miss the updated statistic when the animation completes.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner does not announce banner display
issuesFound.explanation: The _showBanner function displays the consent dialog and traps focus but does not announce its appearance via the live region. Screen reader users may not realize a new dialog has appeared if they were not focused on the trigger.

fpTitles: statCounterAnimator._animate does not announce final value
cookieConsent._showBanner does not announce banner display

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The function marks a form field as invalid by linking an error message via `aria-describedby`, but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field element. Without this attribute, screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the `markInvalid` function before the call to `addDescribedBy`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function toggles the mobile navigation drawer visible and emits an event, but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` in the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: While the `open()` function announces "Navigation menu opened" via `liveRegion.announce`, the `close()` function accepts an `announceToSR` parameter but never uses it to announce when the menu closes. Users relying on screen readers may not realize the menu has dismissed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }` before the `emit` call in the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the `hidden` attribute and adds CSS classes, but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state of the disclosure widget.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;` in the `_openInstance` function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion close function accepts an `announceToSR` parameter but does not use it to announce the collapsed state via the live region, unlike the `open()` function which announces the expanded state. This creates an asymmetry in user feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60))); }` before the `emit` call in the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons function as toggle buttons (remaining selected when active) but only receive a CSS active class via `toggleClass`. They lack `aria-pressed`, which is required for screen readers to convey the selected/toggled state of the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the `forEach` loop in `_syncTabAriaStates`, after the `toggleClass` call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes, there is no auditory feedback via the live region to inform screen reader users that the menu has collapsed and they have returned to the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');` (or appropriate STRINGS key) before the `emit` call in the `_closeInstance` function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion open function expands the answer panel using `animateHeight` and updates CSS classes, but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button to "true", leaving screen reader users unaware that content is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;` in the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation module declares a `_trap` variable intended for focus management but never initializes it with `createFocusTrap()`. Consequently, keyboard focus is not trapped within the open mobile menu, allowing users to tab out into the background content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In the `open` function, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` and ensure `_trap.deactivate()` is called in `close()`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button's `aria-pressed` state is only set when `play()` or `pause()` is called. If the carousel starts in a paused state (due to reduced motion preference or configuration), the button lacks the initial `aria-pressed="false"` attribute, leaving its state ambiguous to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After selecting `_playBtn` in `init`, add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing));` to ensure the initial state is correctly reflected.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid without this state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to false, creating a state mismatch for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function sets it to false, leaving the open state unannounced.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question button. The close function sets it to false, causing an inconsistent state for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close does not announce state change to screen readers

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState uses aria-checked on button elements
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function sets aria-checked on the toggle element regardless of its tag. If the element is a <button>, it should use aria-pressed instead of aria-checked to be valid.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onDecline does not announce declination to screen readers

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the markValid function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to screen readers even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1194: Add removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); after the aria-describedby cleanup logic.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function not updating aria-expanded on trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the faqAccordion module expands the FAQ answer panel and manages the hidden attribute, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). While the close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", the open function omits the corresponding attribute update, leaving screen readers unaware that the content is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2895: Add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after item.open = true;

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters not announcing results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates in the DOM, but there is no announcement via an aria-live region to inform screen reader users how many products are now showing or that the filter action completed successfully.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2205: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but it does not initialise aria-expanded="false" when the page loads with the suggestion panel closed. While the show and hide functions update this attribute dynamically, the initial absence means screen readers do not know the input controls a collapsible region until the user first interacts with it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2522: Add setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod not announcing billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing periods, the price values update visually, but the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users navigating with assistive technology may not realize the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2755: Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`); after _syncToggleState(period);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick not announcing scroll action
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus moves to the skip link, but there is no aria-live announcement confirming the action. Users may be uncertain whether the button worked, especially if the focus management is not immediately apparent.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2032: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page'); before or after the setTimeout focus call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open and close functions not updating aria-expanded on toggle button

The functions manage CSS classes and focus traps but omit the ARIA state attribute that communicates visibility to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and closes without updating the aria-expanded attribute on the hamburger trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce whether the navigation is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization

issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.init(), after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion open and close actions do not announce state changes to screen readers via the live region, even though the functions accept an announceToSR parameter that is never used for live region updates.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In faqAccordion.open(), before `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', ...);`: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : `${item.question} expanded`);`. Line: In faqAccordion.close(), before `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', ...);`: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : `${item.question} collapsed`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls

issuesFound.fix: Line in Phase 1 fade out block: add `el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` alongside the opacity change to ensure elements are hidden from assistive technologies immediately.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
